title: cratoxylum cochinchinense
intro: Cratoxylum cochinchinense, also known as the forest toona tree or tree of life, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its leaves and bark. The health benefits of Cratoxylum cochinchinense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antidiabetic and anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and bark are also used in traditional remedies for various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
